items.q: Which type of nuclear radiation is most ionising?
items.correct: Alpha radiation — large, doubly charged particles cause the most ionisation per unit distance.
items.misconception: Gamma is most ionising because it can travel furthest and pass through almost anything.
items.random_wrong: Beta radiation is most ionising because it consists of fast electrons.
items.why: Students conflate penetrating power with ionising power — gamma penetrates most because it ionises least, the opposite of intuition.

items.q: A book on a high shelf has lots of energy in its:
items.why: Correct answer: gravitational store. “kinetic store” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Petrol stores energy in which form?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: chemical. “thermal” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: How many joules of work are done when a 10 N force moves an object 3 m?
items.why: Exact answer: 30 J. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
